concepts.name: Sets and their representation
concepts.description: Formal definition of a set, types of sets (finite, infinite, singleton, null), and all standard representation methods including roster, set-builder, and Venn diagram visualization for precise understanding.

concepts.name: Union, intersection and complement of sets
concepts.description: Exact definitions, properties, and operations on sets including union, intersection, difference, and complement. Emphasis on Venn diagrams, laws, and practical problem applications.

concepts.name: Algebraic properties of sets
concepts.description: All fundamental set operation laws: commutative, associative, distributive, identity, idempotent, absorption, and De Morgan’s laws with examples for problem-solving.

concepts.name: Power set
concepts.description: Definition, cardinality formula, and properties of the power set. Applications in combinatorics and objective-type problems.

concepts.name: Relations
concepts.description: Definition of a relation as a subset of Cartesian product, types of relations (reflexive, symmetric, transitive, equivalence, partial order), and representation through matrices and digraphs.

concepts.name: Equivalence relations
concepts.description: Equivalence relation properties, identification criteria, equivalence classes, and partitioning sets with rigorous examples.

concepts.name: Functions
concepts.description: Precise definition of a function, domain, codomain, range, mapping rules, and problem-solving framework.

concepts.name: One-one, into and onto functions
concepts.description: Injective, surjective, bijective function definitions with clear identification methods, tests, and illustrative examples.

concepts.name: Composition of functions
concepts.description: Definition, evaluation, domain restrictions, and properties of composite functions, with application in problem-solving and functional equations.

concepts.name: Complex numbers as ordered pairs
concepts.description: Complex numbers defined as (a, b), correspondence with a + ib, and geometric interpretation.

concepts.name: Algebra of complex numbers
concepts.description: All operations: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and conjugation with standard problem-solving applications.

concepts.name: Argand diagram
concepts.description: Geometrical representation in complex plane, plotting, modulus, argument, and vector interpretation.

concepts.name: Modulus of a complex number
concepts.description: Magnitude of complex numbers, properties, inequalities, and practical computation for problem-solving.

concepts.name: Argument of a complex number
concepts.description: Definition, principal argument, properties, and use in representing and solving complex number problems.

concepts.name: Quadratic equations
concepts.description: General quadratic equation in real and complex domains, formulation, and standard problem-solving framework.

concepts.name: Solutions of quadratic equations
concepts.description: Methods: factorization, completing the square, quadratic formula, and discriminant-based analysis.

concepts.name: Relations between roots and coefficients
concepts.description: Exact relations between sum and product of roots with coefficients, applied in equations construction and verification.

concepts.name: Nature of roots
concepts.description: Classification using discriminant: real distinct, real equal, complex roots, with problem-solving strategies.

concepts.name: Formation of quadratic equations
concepts.description: Systematic formation from given roots, sum/product of roots, and applied problem examples.

concepts.name: Matrices
concepts.description: Definition, types (row, column, square, diagonal, zero, identity), order, and representation methods.

concepts.name: Algebra of matrices
concepts.description: Operations: addition, subtraction, multiplication, scalar multiplication, and properties.

concepts.name: Determinants
concepts.description: Definition, evaluation of 2×2 and 3×3 determinants, and properties for simplification.

concepts.name: Evaluation of determinants
concepts.description: Systematic evaluation using cofactor expansion, row/column operations, and problem-solving shortcuts.

concepts.name: Area of triangle using determinants
concepts.description: Application to coordinate geometry to calculate triangle area using determinant formula.

concepts.name: Adjoint of a matrix
concepts.description: Definition, calculation of adjoint using cofactors, and use in finding inverses.

concepts.name: Inverse of a matrix
concepts.description: Calculation using adjoint and determinant, with conditions and examples for solving linear systems.

concepts.name: Test of consistency
concepts.description: Consistency criteria for systems of linear equations using determinant and rank methods.

concepts.name: Solution of linear equations
concepts.description: Methods using inverse matrix, Cramer’s rule, and Gaussian elimination for exact solutions.

concepts.name: Fundamental principle of counting
concepts.description: Sequential event counting rule and application in problem decomposition.

concepts.name: Permutations
concepts.description: Arrangement of objects where order matters, including circular and constrained arrangements.

concepts.name: Combinations
concepts.description: Selection of objects where order doesn’t matter, and applications to binomial problems.

concepts.name: Meaning of P(n, r)
concepts.description: Permutation notation, meaning, formula derivation, and problem-solving applications.

concepts.name: Meaning of C(n, r)
concepts.description: Combination notation, meaning, formula, and applications in counting problems.

concepts.name: Binomial theorem
concepts.description: Precise statement for (a+b)^n, derivation, and expansion with examples for problem-solving.

concepts.name: General term
concepts.description: Formula, identification, and manipulation of general term in binomial expansions.

concepts.name: Middle term
concepts.description: Calculation and application of middle term(s) in expansions.

concepts.name: Simple applications
concepts.description: Coefficient identification, sums, and problem-solving based on binomial theorem.

concepts.name: Arithmetic progression
concepts.description: Definition, general term, sum formula, and properties.

concepts.name: Geometric progression
concepts.description: Definition, general term, sum formulas, and problem-solving tips.

concepts.name: Insertion of arithmetic means
concepts.description: Methods for inserting arithmetic means between two numbers with examples.

concepts.name: Insertion of geometric means
concepts.description: Methods for inserting geometric means with illustrative problems.

concepts.name: Relation between A.M. and G.M.
concepts.description: Inequality relation, derivation, and problem-solving applications.

concepts.name: Trigonometric functions
concepts.description: Definitions of sin, cos, tan, cosec, sec, cot, domains, ranges, and fundamental properties.

concepts.name: Trigonometric identities
concepts.description: All essential identities including Pythagorean, sum-difference, double-angle, half-angle, and product-to-sum formulas for direct problem-solving.

concepts.name: Inverse trigonometric functions
concepts.description: Definitions, principal values, properties, and applications in solving equations.

concepts.name: Real-valued functions
concepts.description: Functions with real outputs, domains, codomains, and properties for analysis.

concepts.name: Algebra of functions
concepts.description: Operations including sum, difference, product, quotient, and composite functions.

concepts.name: Types of functions
concepts.description: Polynomial, rational, trigonometric, logarithmic, exponential with analysis and examples.

concepts.name: Graphs of functions
concepts.description: Plotting, behavior, and transformations of simple functions.

concepts.name: Limits
concepts.description: Rigorous evaluation of limits, L’Hospital’s rule, and problem-solving strategies.

concepts.name: Continuity
concepts.description: Definition, types, and identification of continuous functions with examples.

concepts.name: Differentiability
concepts.description: Definition, tests, and relationship with continuity.

concepts.name: Rules of differentiation
concepts.description: Derivative of sum, difference, product, quotient, and chain rules with examples.

concepts.name: Differentiation of standard functions
concepts.description: Trigonometric, inverse trigonometric, logarithmic, and exponential functions.

concepts.name: Composite and implicit differentiation
concepts.description: Techniques for complex function differentiation and implicit functions.

concepts.name: Second order derivatives
concepts.description: Definition and application in concavity, points of inflection, and acceleration.

concepts.name: Applications of derivatives
concepts.description: Rate of change, monotonicity, maxima, minima, and problem-solving strategies.

concepts.name: Indefinite integrals
concepts.description: Integration as inverse of differentiation, rules, and standard formulas.

concepts.name: Fundamental integrals
concepts.description: Integration of algebraic, trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions.

concepts.name: Integration by substitution
concepts.description: Change of variables for solving integrals efficiently.

concepts.name: Integration by parts
concepts.description: Formula derivation and application for complex integrals.

concepts.name: Integration by partial fractions
concepts.description: Method to integrate rational functions using decomposition into partial fractions.

concepts.name: Integration using trigonometric identities
concepts.description: Utilizing trig identities to simplify and evaluate integrals.

concepts.name: Definite integrals
concepts.description: Evaluation of integrals with limits and properties, applications to area and total change.

concepts.name: Fundamental theorem of calculus
concepts.description: Exact relation between differentiation and integration, applied to problem-solving.

concepts.name: Area under curves
concepts.description: Computation of area bounded by curves using definite integrals.

concepts.name: Differential equations
concepts.description: Order, degree, general and particular solutions, and types of differential equations.

concepts.name: Separation of variables
concepts.description: Technique for solving first-order separable differential equations.

concepts.name: Homogeneous differential equations
concepts.description: Methods to solve homogeneous equations using substitution and simplification.

concepts.name: Linear differential equations
concepts.description: Solution of first-order and higher-order linear differential equations using standard methods.

concepts.name: Cartesian coordinate system
concepts.description: Rectangular coordinate system, axes, quadrants, and plotting points.

concepts.name: Distance formula
concepts.description: Exact formula for distance between two points in 2D space.

concepts.name: Section formula
concepts.description: Internal and external division formulas and examples.

concepts.name: Locus
concepts.description: Definition, equation of a locus, and application in problem-solving.

concepts.name: Straight lines
concepts.description: Equation forms: slope-intercept, point-slope, general; properties and intersection points.

concepts.name: Angle between lines
concepts.description: Formula, derivation, and geometric interpretation.

concepts.name: Distance of a point from a line
concepts.description: Perpendicular distance formula and applications.

concepts.name: Circle
concepts.description: Equation, radius, diameter, tangent, chord properties, and applications.

concepts.name: Parabola
concepts.description: Standard forms, vertex, focus, directrix, axis, and problem-solving applications.

concepts.name: Ellipse
concepts.description: Equation, axes, foci, eccentricity, and applications.

concepts.name: Hyperbola
concepts.description: Equation, axes, foci, eccentricity, asymptotes, and applications.

concepts.name: Vectors and scalars
concepts.description: Definitions, examples, and differences.

concepts.name: Vector addition
concepts.description: Graphical and algebraic methods, properties, and applications.

concepts.name: Components of a vector
concepts.description: Resolution in 2D and 3D, unit vectors, and applications.

concepts.name: Scalar product
concepts.description: Dot product, properties, angle between vectors, and projection applications.

concepts.name: Vector product
concepts.description: Cross product, properties, area calculation, and perpendicular vector applications.

concepts.name: Coordinates in space
concepts.description: Position of points in 3D space, coordinate notation, and plotting.

concepts.name: Distance between two points
concepts.description: 3D distance formula, derivation, and problem-solving.

concepts.name: Direction ratios and direction cosines
concepts.description: Line orientation in space, computation, and applications.

concepts.name: Angle between lines 3D
concepts.description: 3D angle computation using dot product of direction ratios.

concepts.name: Equation of a line
concepts.description: Vector, symmetric, and parametric forms with examples.

concepts.name: Skew lines
concepts.description: Definition, shortest distance formula, and problem-solving methods.

concepts.description: Calculation for grouped and ungrouped data, weighted mean, and applications.

concepts.name: Median
concepts.description: Determination using direct formula or cumulative frequency method.

concepts.description: Determination using formula, histogram interpretation, and applications.

concepts.name: Variance
concepts.description: Definition, formula, and computation for sample and population.

concepts.name: Standard deviation

concepts.name: Mean deviation
concepts.description: Mean deviation about mean or median, calculation, and application.

concepts.description: Definition, properties, sample space, and classical probability calculation.

concepts.description: Union of events, mutually exclusive and non-exclusive cases.

concepts.description: Conditional probability, independent events, and applications.

concepts.description: Formula derivation and application in conditional probability problems.

concepts.name: Random variable
concepts.description: Discrete random variables, probability mass function, expectation, and variance.

concepts.name: Probability distribution
concepts.description: Discrete distributions, expected value, variance, and cumulative distribution functions.

concepts.name: Physical quantities and units
concepts.description: Exhaustive definition of physical quantities with complete SI system coverage, fundamental and derived units, and dimensional consistency rules.

concepts.name: Measurement techniques
concepts.description: All standard measurement methods, least count calculation, significant figures, and propagation of measurement errors.

concepts.name: Errors in measurements
concepts.description: Detailed classification of errors: systematic, random, gross, and techniques to minimize and analyze them.

concepts.name: Dimensional analysis
concepts.description: Dimensional formulae of all physical quantities, verification of equations, derivation of relations using dimensional homogeneity.

concepts.name: Frame of reference
concepts.description: Complete understanding of inertial and non-inertial frames, relative motion, and transformations between frames.

concepts.name: Motion in a straight line
concepts.description: Displacement, distance, speed, velocity, and graphical interpretation with position-time and velocity-time analysis.

concepts.name: Uniformly accelerated motion
concepts.description: Equations of motion derivation, analysis of motion graphs, and problem-solving for constant acceleration.

concepts.name: Relative velocity
concepts.description: Vectorial treatment of relative motion in one and two dimensions, including riverboat and train problems.

concepts.name: Motion in a plane
concepts.description: Projectile motion, range, maximum height, time of flight, and vector decomposition of motion.

concepts.name: Uniform circular motion
concepts.description: Angular displacement, velocity, centripetal acceleration, centripetal force, and dynamics of circular motion.

concepts.description: Comprehensive analysis of inertia, momentum, impulse, and their applications in mechanics.

concepts.description: Detailed treatment of elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions.

concepts.name: Equilibrium of forces
concepts.description: Complete understanding of concurrent and non-concurrent force equilibrium conditions.

concepts.name: Friction
concepts.description: Static, kinetic, rolling friction, laws of friction, and applications in motion analysis.

concepts.name: Circular motion dynamics
concepts.description: Detailed study of centripetal and centrifugal forces, banked curves, and conical pendulum dynamics.

concepts.name: Work by constant and variable forces
concepts.description: Line integral treatment, work done by varying forces, and work-energy principles.

concepts.name: Kinetic and potential energy
concepts.description: Derivation of kinetic and potential energy, work-energy theorem, and practical applications.

concepts.name: Conservation of mechanical energy
concepts.description: Comprehensive treatment of conservative and non-conservative forces and energy transformations.

concepts.name: Motion in a vertical circle
concepts.description: Energy and dynamics analysis of objects in vertical circular paths, including tension and velocity relations.

concepts.name: Collisions
concepts.description: Elastic and inelastic collisions, coefficient of restitution, momentum and energy considerations in 1D and 2D collisions.

concepts.name: Power
concepts.description: Average and instantaneous power, derivations, and problem-solving techniques.

concepts.name: Centre of mass
concepts.description: Determination for two-particle systems and rigid bodies, motion of center of mass, and practical applications.

concepts.name: Rotational kinematics
concepts.description: Angular displacement, velocity, acceleration, and rotational motion equations analogous to linear motion.

concepts.name: Torque and angular momentum
concepts.description: Definition, vector treatment, conservation laws, and applications to rigid bodies.

concepts.name: Moment of inertia
concepts.description: Radius of gyration, derivation for standard bodies, and application in rotational dynamics.

concepts.name: Parallel and perpendicular axis theorems
concepts.description: Derivations and applications to compute moments of inertia of complex bodies.

concepts.name: Equilibrium of rigid bodies
concepts.description: Static and rotational equilibrium conditions, problem-solving with forces and torques.

concepts.name: Universal law of gravitation
concepts.description: Newton’s law of gravitation, derivation, and vector treatment.

concepts.name: Acceleration due to gravity

concepts.description: Derivation from Newton’s laws, orbital mechanics, and application to planetary motion.

concepts.description: Derivation, potential due to system of masses, and escape velocity.

concepts.name: Surface tension
